full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: the paper uses mushrooms, a9a, ijcnn1 etc which are all massive datasets. I need something smaller from sklearn for my reproduction. whats a good binary classification dataset that works well with gaussian kernels?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested Breast Cancer Wisconsin as a good fit — 569 samples, 30 features, binary, commonly used with kernel methods.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Loaded the dataset in sklearn and confirmed it's suitable — binary labels, reasonable feature count, well-studied.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: how do I pick sigma for the gaussian kernel? paper says 5th percentile of pairwise distances. can you give me the numpy/scipy code for that
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Gave me pdist + np.percentile code. Put it into my preprocessing cell with renamed variables.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Ran the code and checked sigma value was reasonable (~2.98 for Breast Cancer). Matched paper's methodology.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: sklearn breast cancer labels are 0 and 1 but the paper needs {-1, +1}. how do I convert? also need 80/20 stratified split
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: y = 2*y_raw - 1 for conversion, train_test_split with stratification. Used this exact logic with my RANDOM_SEED variable.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified label distribution after conversion — balanced classes confirmed.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: need a quick matplotlib plot — histogram of pairwise distances showing where sigma falls, and class distribution bar chart. side by side subplots
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Gave plt.subplots(1,2) code. Changed sigma line to red dashed and added savefig.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Plot looks correct — sigma at 5th percentile clearly visible, class distribution is balanced.
